name: agentcall
summary: Phone numbers, SMS, two-way AI SMS plus relay mode (text your own agent on a real number), proactive scheduling (the agent texts first: appointment reminders and recurring digests), AI voice agents (Managed $0.40/min, BYOK $0.10/min, or Premium Voice $0.59/min on inbound + outbound) with 13 languages plus auto-detect, and Auditable Call Memory (cross-call contacts, structured call reports, the next-call context block) for AI agents. 60 tools. VoIP-routed via licensed US carriers.
description: Phone numbers, SMS, voice calls, inbound + outbound AI voice with multilingual support, AND Auditable Call Memory for AI agents. Provision US/CA numbers, send/receive texts, extract OTP codes for apps you control, configure phone numbers to be answered by an AI voice agent (with plain-English email summaries and optional MP3 recordings after every call), or place autonomous AI-powered outbound calls. Inbound texts can be answered too: set smsMode to 'ai' so AgentCall's AI replies and pulls real quotes by calling tools you host through the action bridge, or set smsMode to 'relay' so AgentCall runs no AI and forwards each text to your own agent and sends its reply back, letting you text your own agent on a real number. AI voice supports 12 languages plus auto-detect on both directions: English, Spanish, French, German, Italian, Portuguese, Dutch, Japanese, Korean, Chinese (Mandarin), Hindi, and Arabic. Default is auto (matches the caller naturally); pin a specific language to keep the AI in that language even if the other party speaks another, useful for businesses serving a specific language community or for outbound errands like booking a Spanish doctor's appointment on behalf of someone. Inbound AI voice ships in two billing modes per number: Managed at $0.40/min (AgentCall holds the AI provider key, simplest setup) or BYOK at $0.10/min (customer provides their own AI provider key, AgentCall bills only the platform overhead). Premium Voice is an opt-in Pro add-on that answers inbound calls with a more natural, brandable voice from a curated library, in all 13 languages, with full caller memory, at $0.59/min only on numbers where it is enabled. Enable it per number from the dashboard or programmatically via the set_premium_voice MCP tool (disable_premium_voice to revert, list_premium_voices to browse). Memory is included on every plan and every voice mode. Every completed AI call auto-extracts into structured, source-backed memory: contacts, facts, preferences, commitments, tasks, briefs. Cross-call memory means a returning caller is recognized and the AI starts with what was said last time. Ask AgentCall what is known about any phone number before placing a call via get_next_call_context. Numbers are VoIP-routed via licensed US carriers and not intended for consumer-platform signup verification (Stripe, WhatsApp, banks, etc.). A number's AI agent can also text first on a schedule: create_schedule sets up one-time appointment reminders or recurring digests, a reply to a proactive text threads back into the same AI conversation, and a calendar integration is just a client of create_schedule. Tools that cost money, contact real people, or wipe memory are explicitly flagged so agents confirm with the user before invoking.
author: agentcall

AGENTCALL_API_KEY.description: AgentCall API key (starts with ac_live_). Get one free at https://agentcall.co

AGENTCALL_BASE_URL.description: Override the API base URL. Defaults to https://api.agentcall.co. Set for staging environments, on-prem deployments, or custom routing setups.

AGENTCALL_TIMEOUT_MS.description: Per-request timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to 30000 (30 seconds). Increase for long-running endpoints like waitForOTP or slow upstream networks.

billing.notice: Free tier: 1 phone number, 10 SMS/mo, 5 call min/mo, 5 inbound AI voice min/mo (no card required for the trial), never charged. Pro ($19.99/mo): unlimited numbers + usage-based pricing (SMS $0.015, voice $0.035/min, AI voice $0.40/min outbound or inbound on the Managed billing mode, no monthly cap on inbound AI). Inbound AI voice is per-number configurable in two billing modes: Managed (default, $0.40/min, AgentCall holds the AI provider key) or BYOK ($0.10/min, customer provides their own AI provider key via set_byok_openai_key and AgentCall bills only the platform overhead). Memory is included on every plan and both voice modes. Once the Free inbound AI trial is exhausted, calls hang up until the 1st of next month (UTC) when the trial resets, OR the user upgrades to Pro. There is no pay-as-you-go overage path on Free. See https://agentcall.co/#pricing
paidFeatures: inbound AI voice past the 5 min/mo Free trial (Pro plan only; Managed $0.40/min or BYOK $0.10/min, no monthly cap)
Premium Voice on inbound + outbound calls ($0.59/min, opt-in per number, Pro add-on; 12 curated voices, 13 languages, full caller memory)
opt-in call recording with dashboard playback ($0.01/min, 1-year retention)

webhooks.notice: This skill can register webhook endpoints to receive real-time events (inbound SMS, OTP codes, call status, recordings, AI call transcripts). Webhooks here are for OUTBOUND event delivery, NOT for routing inbound calls. To make a phone number answer incoming calls, use POST /v1/numbers/:numberId/inbound-config (the AgentCall equivalent of Twilio's inbound voice webhook URL), not webhook registration. Webhooks require an HTTPS URL you control.

changelog: v2.12.0 (2026-06-11): AgentFM callback flow, outbound Premium Voice parity, 60 tools. (1) New request_agentfm_callback tool (tool 60): queues a podcast guest callback to AgentFM, no AgentCall account required, deduplicated per phone per 24 hours. The AI host calls the guest back using outbound premium voice. Mirrors the public POST /v1/agentfm/callback-request endpoint (3 requests/hour/IP rate limit, honeypot, 24h dedup). (2) Outbound Premium Voice: when Premium Voice is enabled on a number (set_premium_voice), POST /v1/calls/ai outbound calls from that number now also use the premium voice at $0.59/min. Previously Premium Voice applied only to inbound calls; both directions are now covered. (3) DB migration: agentfm_callbacks table (RLS enabled, Prisma-only access). (4) Runner script npm run script:agentfm-callbacks (dry-run by default, --apply to dial, --limit N). (5) Public web form at agentcall.co/agentfm. Total tools now 60 (was 59). Ships with @agentcall/mcp-server@0.16.0 and agentcall@0.9.2; hosted MCP at api.agentcall.co/mcp synced in lockstep. Smithery needs manual Publish click. v2.11.0 (2026-06-10): Agent-to-agent calling polish plus four new capabilities, shipped across REST, MCP, and SDK. (1) New synthesize_speech MCP tool and POST /v1/tts endpoint: standalone text to speech in the same 10 voices calls use, mp3 or wav, 4096 chars per request, $0.03 per 1,000 characters, Pro plan; confirm text and approximate cost with the user before large batches. Total tools now 59 (was 58). (2) initiate_ai_call gains metadata (string map, max 24 keys / 2KB, echoed on the call object and every call webhook, copied to the linked agent-to-agent inbound leg) and liveTranscript (each finalized utterance fires the new transcript.partial webhook event during the call: callId, sequence, role, text, timestamp; the post-call call.transcript stays authoritative). (3) Agent-to-agent calls between two numbers on one account now expose peerCallId on both call records and all call webhook payloads. (4) The action bridge is now channel-agnostic in production: the same tools + actionWebhook that power two-way AI SMS let the inbound AI VOICE agent call customer tools mid-call (context.channel "voice", callId populated, fail-soft on errors); the per-call count ships as toolCallCount on the call record and report. Also: canonical call status vocabulary everywhere (initiated, ringing, in-progress, completed, busy, no_answer, failed), the call report is delivered in one canonical shape on both GET /v1/calls/:id/report (report) and the call.report.ready webhook (data.report) with facts[].quote (the raw payload field is deprecated for one release), and call recording on inbound AI numbers is fixed end-to-end (record_start timing, recording storage, and per-minute billing all verified live). Ships with @agentcall/mcp-server@0.15.0 and agentcall@0.9.2; hosted MCP at api.agentcall.co/mcp synced in lockstep. v2.10.0 (2026-06-08): Proactive scheduling. A number's AI agent can now text first on a schedule: one-time appointment reminders and recurring digests. Three new MCP tools: create_schedule (numberId, contactPhone, exactly one of fireAt | recurrence, exactly one of template | promptHint, optional payload, timezone, dedupeKey), list_schedules, cancel_schedule. New REST endpoints POST /v1/numbers/:id/schedules, GET /v1/numbers/:id/schedules, DELETE /v1/numbers/:id/schedules/:scheduleId. fireAt is a one-time ISO 8601 instant; recurrence is { frequency 'daily' | 'weekly', hour, minute, dayOfWeek } interpreted in an IANA timezone. template is sent verbatim with {{placeholders}} filled from payload; promptHint lets the agent compose the message from its memory of the contact. dedupeKey makes a re-create return 409 so a calendar sync never duplicates a reminder. A reply to a proactive message threads into the same two-way AI SMS conversation, so a one-word answer is understood in context. Pro plan, the number must have smsMode 'ai', each send is billed as one outbound text. Total tools now 58 (was 55). Ships with @agentcall/mcp-server@0.14.0 and agentcall@0.9.1; hosted MCP at api.agentcall.co/mcp synced in lockstep. v2.9.0 (2026-06-06): Two-way AI SMS and relay mode, plus three SMS conversation tools. configure_inbound_ai gains six optional fields: smsMode ('off' | 'ai' | 'relay'), smsSystemPrompt, actionWebhook, tools, agentWebhook, allowedSenders. With smsMode 'ai', inbound texts to the number get an AI reply that follows the prompt, remembers the contact across voice and SMS, and can call customer-declared tools through the action bridge (actionWebhook, HMAC-signed, the same security model as the pre-call context webhook) to return real values instead of guessing; managed-only, Pro, billed as one outbound text per reply. With smsMode 'relay', AgentCall runs no LLM of its own and forwards each inbound text to the customer's own agent at agentWebhook (HMAC-signed); the agent replies on its own schedule, so a user can text their own agent on a real number; allowedSenders (E.164, max 20) restricts which numbers can reach the agent; billed as plain texts, no relay fee. STOP is honored before either mode runs and both are inbound-only in v1. Three new MCP tools: list_sms_conversations and get_sms_conversation read the threads; reply_to_sms_conversation (conversationId, body, optional idempotencyKey) sends the relay reply, is opt-out-checked, and is idempotent for 24 hours so a retried agent never double-texts. New REST endpoints GET /v1/sms-conversations, GET /v1/sms-conversations/:id, POST /v1/sms-conversations/:id/reply. New webhook event sms.ai_reply fires on each AI reply. Total tools now 55 (was 52). Ships with @agentcall/mcp-server@0.13.0 and agentcall@0.9.0; hosted MCP at api.agentcall.co/mcp synced in lockstep. v2.8.2 (2026-06-05): The configure_inbound_ai notify block gains an optional smsTo field so the owner can also receive a one-line call summary by text in addition to the email summary (US notification numbers, one SMS per call, spam skipped, reply STOP to disable, billed as one outbound SMS at $0.015). No new tools (still 52). Ships with @agentcall/mcp-server@0.12.2 and agentcall@0.8.2; hosted MCP at api.agentcall.co/mcp synced in lockstep. v2.8.1 (2026-06-02): Patch release from a post-ship review pass. No tool, schema, or behavior changes (still 52 tools). SDK PremiumVoice.gender tightened to the 'female' | 'male' union, the setPremiumVoice / disablePremiumVoice docstrings now document the 404 number_not_found error, and the npm server startup banner reports the correct tool count. Ships with @agentcall/mcp-server@0.12.1 and agentcall@0.8.1; hosted MCP at api.agentcall.co/mcp synced in lockstep. v2.8.0 (2026-06-02): Agents can now enable Premium Voice, not just browse it. Three new MCP tools: set_premium_voice (numberId, voiceId) enables Premium Voice on a number or changes its premium voice; disable_premium_voice (numberId) reverts to the standard voice; list_premium_voices returns the catalog. set_premium_voice is a billable Pro add-on ($0.59/min) so its description instructs the agent to confirm the exact numberId, the chosen voice, and the $0.59/min rate with the user before invoking, and to surface the 403 (not Pro) / 402 (no card) / 409 (inbound AI not configured) errors rather than retry. Requires the number to already have inbound AI configured; never wipes the rest of the inbound config; idempotent. Mirrors the existing dashboard self-serve flow and the new SDK methods client.numbers.setPremiumVoice / disablePremiumVoice and client.calls.premiumVoices. Total tools now 52 (was 49). Ships with @agentcall/mcp-server@0.12.0 and agentcall@0.8.0; hosted MCP at api.agentcall.co/mcp synced in lockstep. v2.7.1 (2026-06-02): Documented the public Premium Voice catalog endpoint in the Browse Voices section so agents can list premium voices the same way they list standard ones: GET /v1/calls/premium-voices (no auth) returns { voices: [{ id, name, description, gender, accent, sampleUrl }], pricing }. Each id is the voiceId to enable per number. v2.7.0 (2026-06-02): Premium Voice. Inbound AI receptionists can now answer with Premium Voice (powered by ElevenLabs): a curated library of 12 more natural, brandable voices, all 13 languages, and full caller memory, billed at $0.59/min only on the numbers where it is enabled. The 10 standard voices stay the default at $0.40/min, so the right voice is chosen per number. Premium Voice is enabled self-serve per number from the dashboard (a number's inbound AI settings); preview the catalog at the public GET /v1/calls/premium-voices endpoint (no auth). update_number_voice now describes the Premium Voice tier. As a billable add-on, confirm the user accepts the $0.59/min premium rate on the specific number before enabling. Ships with @agentcall/mcp-server@0.11.0; hosted MCP at api.agentcall.co/mcp synced in lockstep. v2.6.0 (2026-05-25): Two new optional input fields on initiate_ai_call (POST /v1/calls/ai). useSavedAgent: boolean hydrates systemPrompt, voice, language, firstMessage, maxDurationSecs, and record from the saved outbound agent on the from-number (set via set_outbound_defaults) so CSV runners don't re-spread the prompt on every row; per-call overrides win, so firstMessage can stay personalized per recipient while inheriting the saved persona. Returns code no_saved_agent if the from-number has no saved outbound agent. idempotencyKey: string (1 to 200 chars) scoped per phone number; a duplicate request with the same from + idempotencyKey pair replays the original response (with header X-AgentCall-Idempotency-Replayed: true) and does not place a second carrier call. Uniqueness is enforced at the database layer so concurrent retries are race-safe. The canonical CSV-runner row is {from, to, useSavedAgent: true, idempotencyKey: 'batch-id:row-id', firstMessage: '...'}. Tool count remains 49. Ships with @agentcall/mcp-server@0.10.0 and agentcall@0.7.0; hosted MCP at api.agentcall.co/mcp synced in lockstep. v2.5.0 (2026-05-25): Added set_outbound_defaults, get_outbound_defaults, clear_outbound_defaults tools. Save a reusable outbound AI agent (systemPrompt, voice, language, firstMessage, maxDurationSecs, record, optional templateId) on a phone number so the dashboard Place AI call dialog and any MCP client can hydrate from it instead of retyping for every outbound call from the same number. Pro-only. Independent of the inbound AI receptionist on the same number. The `outbound` field is now also returned on every list_numbers and get_number response alongside the existing `inbound` field. Total tools now 49 (was 46). Ships with @agentcall/mcp-server@0.9.0; hosted MCP at api.agentcall.co/mcp synced in lockstep. v2.4.1 (2026-05-26): New follow-up-on-call MCP prompt. Walks the agent through calling back the person from a previous inbound call: load the original call detail, load the structured report (intent, summary, commitments, tasks, unresolved questions, spam flag, caller name), confirm with the user, then place an outbound AI call with a stitched prompt grounded in what the caller actually said. Stops on spam-flagged calls. Optional `reason` parameter lets the user pin a specific follow-up purpose (e.g. 'tell them their estimate is ready') instead of letting the AI infer from open items. Mirrors the new 'Follow up with AI' button on the dashboard call detail page so agents on Claude Desktop, Cursor, and OpenClaw have parity with the dashboard surface. Total prompts now 5 (was 4). Ships with @agentcall/mcp-server@0.8.1; hosted MCP at api.agentcall.co/mcp synced in lockstep. v2.4.0 (2026-05-25): Multilingual AI voice on inbound and outbound. New optional `language` field on configure_inbound_ai and initiate_ai_call accepts 'auto' (default, matches the caller naturally) plus 12 ISO-639-1 codes: en, es, fr, de, it, pt, nl, ja, ko, zh, hi, ar. When a specific code is set, the runtime prepends a 'respond exclusively in {language}' directive to the system prompt at session-build time and pins the speech-to-text language hint for cleaner transcription. The customer's systemPrompt stays unchanged so swapping language never wipes the prompt. PATCH /v1/numbers/:id now accepts language alongside label and voice for partial updates. New MCP tool update_number_language mirrors update_number_voice for language-only edits. Three new outbound prompt templates ('outbound-appointment-booker', 'outbound-info-request', 'outbound-callback-confirmation') with placeholders for booking-on-behalf-of use cases. The /v1/calls/prompt-templates endpoint now accepts an optional ?direction=inbound or ?direction=outbound query filter. The firstMessage stays verbatim regardless of language setting, so callers should translate the greeting themselves if non-English. Total tools now 45 (was 44). Ships with @agentcall/mcp-server@0.8.0 and agentcall@0.5.0; hosted MCP at api.agentcall.co/mcp synced in lockstep. v2.3.1 (2026-05-19): Schema-drift fix on 5 MCP tool output shapes. Customers using Claude Desktop and Cursor occasionally saw validation errors on calls that completed successfully; this release aligns the declared shapes with the live API responses. webhook rotate now returns the full Webhook row instead of just secret; webhook create includes isActive in the response; get_call_report candidates expose reportId, contactId, resolvedAt, resolvedBy; get_call_transcript returns 202 for in-progress calls so it doesn't collide with the documented 200 transcript shape; PATCH /v1/memory/:id projects the updated row to the declared memory shape. Plus drops 4 top-level BYOK status fields from inboundAiConfigOutputSchema (voiceMode, hasByokKey, byokOpenaiApiKeyPreview, byokConfiguredAt). These have always lived inside `config` and were incorrectly declared at the top level. SDK consumers now read these as result.config.hasByokKey etc. Ships with @agentcall/mcp-server@0.7.1 and agentcall@0.4.1. v2.3.0 (2026-05-18): added set_byok_openai_key + disable_byok tools. configure_inbound_ai response now exposes voiceMode + hasByokKey + byokOpenaiApiKeyPreview. Inbound AI voice is per-number configurable in two billing modes: Managed ($0.40/min, AgentCall holds the AI provider key) or BYOK ($0.10/min, customer provides their own AI provider key). Memory is included on both modes and on every plan. Total tools now 44. Ships with @agentcall/mcp-server@0.7.0 and the hosted MCP at api.agentcall.co/mcp synced in lockstep. v2.2.3 (2026-05-16): Customer-controlled cross-call memory toggle. New PATCH /v1/account endpoint accepting { memoryEnabled: boolean } so customers can enable or disable Auditable Call Memory themselves from the dashboard or programmatically. GET /v1/account now returns memoryEnabled. SDK adds client.account.get() and client.account.setMemoryEnabled(true|false). New MCP tool set_memory_enabled (43 tools total, was 42). Available on Free + Pro; Free volume is naturally capped by the 5-min/month inbound AI trial so extraction cost stays trivial. Toggling off does NOT delete existing memory; use purge_contact for that. Ships with @agentcall/mcp-server@0.6.3 and agentcall@0.3.1. v2.2.2 (2026-05-16): Smithery quality score work. Added two optional config entries to credentials (AGENTCALL_BASE_URL, AGENTCALL_TIMEOUT_MS) wired through to the AgentCall SDK so MCP-marketplace listings show a richer configuration UX. Swept 9 input-parameter descriptions that were missing on the new memory tools (update_contact, update_memory, list_call_reports, list_briefs) so every tool's input fields are fully documented. No tool behavior change. Ships with @agentcall/mcp-server@0.6.2 and the hosted MCP at api.agentcall.co/mcp synced. v2.2.1 (2026-05-16): Tightened the outputSchema on two memory tools (get_call_report.payload and get_memory_timeline.events[].before/after) from z.unknown() to fully-typed Zod object shapes mirroring the actual API response. Field-level descriptions added on the rest of the CallReport surface. No behavior change; tool callers see the same JSON. Ships with @agentcall/mcp-server@0.6.1 and the hosted MCP at api.agentcall.co/mcp synced in the same release. v2.2.0 (2026-05-16): Auditable Call Memory feature. Every completed AI call auto-extracts into a structured CallReport plus MemoryCandidate rows: facts, preferences, decisions, commitments, tasks, risks, the nextCallContext paragraph, an ownerBrief block when the call needs attention. High-confidence candidates auto-promote into Memory (Current Truth) via a per-agent policy; lower-confidence and decision/task candidates stay proposed for manual review. The Bob-vs-Robert handler resolves same-slot contradictions: corroborate on same text, supersede on >0.15 confidence delta, otherwise keep existing and reject the new candidate. Every change writes a MemoryEvent so the audit trail is queryable. 20 new endpoints: GET /v1/contacts, GET /v1/contacts/:id, GET /v1/contacts/by-phone/:phone, GET /v1/contacts/:id/next-call-context (the killer tool: render the Memory block the AI would receive on the next call), GET /v1/memory/current, GET /v1/memory/:id, GET /v1/memory/:id/timeline, GET /v1/memory/candidates, GET /v1/reports, GET /v1/calls/:id/report, GET /v1/briefs, PATCH /v1/contacts/:id, DELETE /v1/contacts/:id (right-to-forget; cascades Memory + Candidate + Brief; redacts CallReport.payload), PATCH /v1/memory/:id, DELETE /v1/memory/:id, POST /v1/memory/candidates/:id/approve, POST /v1/memory/candidates/:id/reject, POST /v1/briefs/:id/ack, POST /v1/briefs/:id/resolve. 10 new MCP tools mirror the read surface (list_contacts, get_contact, get_contact_by_phone, get_next_call_context, get_current_memory, get_memory, get_memory_timeline, list_memory_candidates, list_call_reports, get_call_report); 9 new MCP tools mirror the write surface (update_contact, purge_contact, update_memory, delete_memory, approve_memory_candidate, reject_memory_candidate, list_briefs, acknowledge_brief, resolve_brief). purge_contact and delete_memory carry destructiveHint=true so MCP clients prompt for confirmation. Hosted MCP at api.agentcall.co/mcp and the npm @agentcall/mcp-server bumped in lockstep to 0.6.0 with 42 tools total. New webhook event call.report.ready fires after the extractor completes, ~5 to 30 seconds after the call ends, alongside the existing call.transcript event. Inbound AI configure_inbound_ai now accepts contextSource (none / agentcall_memory / webhook / merge) to route the pre-call context block. SDK agentcall@0.3.0 adds client.contacts.*, client.memory.*, client.reports.*, client.briefs.* plus client.calls.getReport. Feature is opt-in per agent via Agent.memoryEnabled (default false), so existing customers see zero behavior change until the flag is flipped. v2.1.19 (2026-05-14): Fixed an outputSchema drift on list_calls. The /v1/calls list endpoint deliberately omits `recordingUrl` and substitutes a cheap `hasRecording` boolean per row (avoids minting a signed URL per row, which would burn rate limit on every page fetch). The MCP list_calls outputSchema used the detail-call shape which required `recordingUrl`, so list_calls responses failed strict validation when consumers like Hermes ran them. Now there are two shapes: the detail shape (used by get_call, initiate_call, initiate_ai_call) still has `recordingUrl`; the list shape (used by list_calls) has `hasRecording: boolean` instead. Consumers fetch the actual URL via get_call or `GET /v1/calls/:id/recording`. Ships with @agentcall/mcp-server@0.4.19 and the hosted MCP at api.agentcall.co/mcp updated in the same release. v2.1.18 (2026-05-13): Post-call transcript webhook is documented end-to-end in the Webhooks section. Subscribe to the `call.transcript` event to receive the full transcript and LLM-extracted summary (callerName, intent, urgency, callbackBy, spam) after every inbound AI call ends. Payload is HMAC-SHA256 signed in the `X-AgentCall-Signature` header. Receiving endpoints verify the signature, dedup on `callId`, and acknowledge with 2xx as soon as the payload is persisted; AgentCall retries with exponential backoff on non-2xx. Three integration patterns are documented (store, forward, queue-for-local-agent). Reference public bridge github.com/Kintupercy/agentcall-hermes-bridge v0.2.0 now exposes `POST /agentcall/transcript` (HMAC-verified queue append, bounded at 100) and `POST /hermes/pull-transcripts` (read-and-clear) for agent platforms that run locally and can't accept inbound HTTPS. Full walkthrough at https://agentcall.co/docs/post-call-webhook. v2.1.17 (2026-05-13): configure_inbound_ai now officially accepts an optional `contextWebhook` block alongside the existing fields. When set, AgentCall POSTs to your HTTPS URL on every inbound call connect (HMAC-signed); the response's contextBlock string is merged onto the systemPrompt before the AI answers. Use this to inject a live brief, current priorities, or recent emails so the AI speaks with up-to-date context instead of a static prompt. Fail-open: any webhook error leaves the call running with the static prompt. Previously this field was settable only via the inboundConfig JSON directly; surfaced now on POST /v1/numbers/:id/inbound-config and the SDK. v2.1.16 (2026-05-13): Expanded the voice catalog labels embedded in MCP tool descriptions so agents see a trait and a best-fit use case for every voice (previously only marin, cedar, and shimmer had inline labels; the remaining seven were bare names). Applies to configure_inbound_ai, update_number_voice, initiate_ai_call, and the ai-outbound-call prompt. The /v1/calls/voices endpoint already returned full labels for all ten voices; this just brings the embedded tool descriptions in line. v2.1.15 (2026-05-13): Added a partial-update path on PATCH /v1/numbers/:id so agents can change just the inbound AI voice (or just the label) without touching the rest of the inbound config. Use this when the user asks to switch voices on an existing receptionist; the systemPrompt, firstMessage, recording flag, and notify email block are preserved. The full-replace POST /v1/numbers/:id/inbound-config path is still the right tool for first-time setup or when changing multiple fields at once. v2.1.14 (2026-05-09): Continued ClawScan v2.4.22 'Rogue Agents' work. v2.1.13 added a checklist + monitor/disable obligation but kept an explicit risk-restatement paragraph ('Once you configure inbound AI on a number, that number autonomously answers every incoming call from then on, accumulates per-minute charges, and keeps doing so until someone calls disable_inbound_ai'). The deep LLM scan quoted that paragraph as the Concern, even though it was followed by mitigations: confidence dropped High -> Medium but moderation flipped to suspicious.llm_suspicious. v2.1.14 drops the risk-restatement paragraph and keeps only the procedural content. The section header now mirrors the scanner's recommendation language verbatim ('Enable Only After Explicit User Confirmation; Monitor Usage; Disable When No Longer Needed'). Pre-flight checklist and post-configuration responsibility sections retained. v2.1.13 (2026-05-09): Address ClawScan v2.4.22 'Rogue Agents' finding (severity High, status Concern) on the v2.1.12 detail page. The scanner correctly flagged that configure_inbound_ai is a persistent configuration: once enabled, the number autonomously answers every incoming call and accrues $0.40/min Pro charges (or hangs up at the 5-minute Free trial cap) until disable_inbound_ai is called. The previous Cost & Safety section covered billable actions generically but didn't spell out the persistent-after-configuration risk or a monitor/disable obligation. Added a dedicated 'Persistent Inbound AI Configuration: Confirm Before Enabling, Monitor After' subsection with a 6-item pre-config checklist (exact number, system prompt readback, recording flag, notify email, maxDurationSecs, budget expectations) and a post-config monitoring + disable obligation. Mirrors the v2.1.4 -> v2.1.5 'Tool Misuse and Exploitation' fix pattern. Also: full em-dash sweep on instructions.md and SKILL.md (41 -> 0) per Percy's no-em-dash style rule, since the file picked up new dashes during the v2.1.5/v2.1.6/v2.1.12 edits. v2.1.12 (2026-05-09): Voice catalog expanded from 8 to 10 voices. Added marin (soft, natural) and cedar (warm, grounded) as the newest natural-sounding picks, ideal for receptionist, support, and trust-building conversations. Both are live-preview only for now (configure on a number and call to hear them); the original 8 still ship with sample MP3s via GET /v1/calls/voices. Also fixed a silent-greeting bug on the new voice infrastructure: AI receptionists now greet callers immediately instead of waiting for the caller to speak first. Removed remaining vendor mentions from public-facing copy in skill instructions and SDK docs (the integration is what matters; the underlying provider isn't user-relevant). v2.1.11 (2026-05-09): Free inbound AI trial is now strictly trial-only (no pay-as-you-go overage on Free). Once the 5 minutes/month are used up, additional inbound AI calls hang up until the trial resets on the 1st of next month (UTC), OR the user must upgrade to Pro for unlimited inbound AI. Cleaner trial-to-upgrade funnel, matches how successful trial-driven SaaS works (Loom, Notion, Linear). Updated billing notice and paidFeatures: removed the 'add a card to keep going at $0.40/min' messaging since that path no longer exists; clarified inbound AI past the trial is Pro-only. v2.1.10 (2026-05-08): Two paidFeatures corrections. (1) Removed 'post-call email summaries' from paidFeatures â€” they fire whenever inbound AI is configured (notify.emailTo set), regardless of plan, so framing them as a paid feature was inaccurate. Free trial users get the same email summaries as Pro for any of their 5 trial-minute calls, just bounded by call volume. (2) Replaced 'unlimited numbers' with 'unlimited numbers, toll-free, mobile types' to surface that Free is now local-only at the API layer (matches the dashboard provision-dialog Pro-only badges and the agentcall.co/#pricing comparison table). Also: minor wording tightening on the inbound AI bullet. v2.1.9 (2026-05-08): Inbound AI voice is now available on the Free tier as a 5-minute monthly trial with no card required. Updated billing notice, paidFeatures, the Cost & Safety section in the skill instructions, and the Inbound AI Voice section heading to reflect the new tier behavior. Outbound AI voice and call recording remain card-on-file Pro features. v2.1.8 (2026-05-07): Removed specific carrier vendor names from public-facing copy. Numbers are now described as VoIP-routed via 'licensed US carriers' (instead of naming the specific underlying providers). Internal infrastructure detail isn't useful product copy and shouldn't be on the public surface. v2.1.7 (2026-05-07): Followup to v2.1.6 false-advertising fix. Set the platform-stored skill summary (was previously stuck on the original Feb 2026 'Give your agent real phone numbers for SMS, OTP verification, and voice calls' line, which implied external-platform OTP verification works). New summary scopes to honest capabilities (AI receptionist + email summaries + programmable phone/SMS, VoIP-routed). Description also expanded to call out 'OTP for apps you control' and explicit non-fit for consumer-platform signup verification. v2.1.6 (2026-05-07): Removed false-advertising claims about SIM-backed numbers. Numbers are VoIP-routed via Telnyx and Twilio, not real SIM cards, and consumer platforms that block VoIP for signup (Stripe, WhatsApp, Google, banks) will reject them. Real eSIM integration is on the roadmap. Dropped the 'sim' type from the provision types list and removed 'SIM numbers' from paidFeatures. v2.1.5 (2026-05-06): Four updates bundled. (1) Added a top-level Cost & Safety section to instructions.md telling the AI agent to confirm with the user before invoking billable, irreversible, or external-effect tools (provision/release numbers, send SMS, start calls). Documents existing rate limits, plan caps, and payment-method gates as baseline guardrails. Addresses the ClawScan v2.4.22 finding on tool misuse / exploitation. (2) Documented opt-in call recording on AI voice paths ($0.01/min, 1-year dashboard retention, signed-URL playback, TCPA auto-disclosure on first message in two-party-consent states). MCP `configure_inbound_ai` and `initiate_ai_call` tools now expose the `record` flag; ships with @agentcall/mcp-server@0.4.2 and agentcall@0.1.5. (3) De-emphasized 'OpenAI Realtime' in user-facing copy â€” the integration (phone numbers, MCP, email summary, dashboard playback) is the value proposition; vendor disclosure stays in technical contexts (error messages, FAQ when directly asked). (4) Removed the stale 'MCP `configure_inbound_ai` doesn't expose `notify` yet' caveat from the inbound-config example â€” both MCP tools (hosted + npm) now accept the notify block, so agents in Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf, etc. configure the email destination in one tool call. v2.1.4 (2026-05-05): Surface the post-call email summary as a Pro deliverable in the description, paidFeatures, and instructions. After every inbound AI call, AgentCall emails the configured address a plain-English summary (caller name, phone, intent, urgency, callback time) â€” no transcript-reading required. Configured via the dashboard's `notify` block; MCP/SDK exposure is on the next minor release. v2.1.3 (2026-05-04): Disambiguate webhooks vs inbound call routing in skill instructions and webhook notice â€” prevents AI assistants (e.g. OpenClaw) from suggesting webhook registration when the user wants `configure_inbound_ai`. Triggered by paying-customer report. v2.1.2 (2026-04-29): Replace internal carrier names with user-friendly geographic phrasing in copy. v2.1.0 (2026-04-29): Added inbound AI voice â€” configure any supported number (US and Canada) to be answered autonomously by an OpenAI Realtime voice agent. Added GET /v1/calls/voices (8-voice catalog with sample MP3s) and GET /v1/calls/prompt-templates (5 ready-made templates: receptionist, lead qualifier, appointment booker, customer support, call screener). Default voice changed from alloy to shimmer (warmer, more welcoming). Updated tollfree pricing to $2.50/mo and mobile to $2/mo (correcting stale rates).
